metadata.title: A stormy afternoon with Little Women
metadata.curiosityNote: Louisa May Alcott wrote Little Women in a few months at her desk in Concord.

story.briefTemplate: Seraphina had read {quantity:pages_before_lunch} of Little Women before lunch. An ice storm rattled the windows and canceled the afternoon outing, so she curled under a blanket and read {quantity:afternoon_pages} more. Across the room, Grandma read {quantity:grandma_pages} from Little Men.
story.causalEvent: The ice storm canceled the outing and gave Seraphina extra reading time.
story.closingNoteTemplate: The reading log closed at {quantity:combined_reading_pages} for the stormy afternoon.

label.child: Extra pages read during the storm
Pages Grandma read from Little Men

steps.prompt: How many total pages did Seraphina read by afternoon?
steps.reasoningPrompt: The storm gave Seraphina extra reading time. Did her page total go up or down?

backwardCheck.prompt: Take the afternoon pages back off. Do you land on the before-lunch count?

steps.prompt: How many pages did Seraphina and Grandma read altogether?
steps.reasoningPrompt: Seraphina's pages and Grandma's pages are two parts of the reading day. How do they make the total?

backwardCheck.prompt: Set Grandma's pages aside. Do you reach Seraphina's total?

operatorExperiments.alternateWorldTemplate: This matches the story: the storm gave {quantity:afternoon_pages} of extra reading that join the morning's pages.
Subtracting would fit a different story where {quantity:afternoon_pages} were skipped instead of read.
This would fit a different question: if there were {value:afternoon_pages} stretches like {label:pages_before_lunch.lowercase}, how many would there be?
This would fit a different question about sharing {label:pages_before_lunch.lowercase} into equal groups.
This matches the story: {label:seraphina_total_pages.lowercase} and {label:grandma_pages.lowercase} combine into the reading log.
Subtracting would fit a different story where {quantity:grandma_pages} were set aside instead of added.
This would fit a different question: if there were {value:grandma_pages} groups like {label:seraphina_total_pages.lowercase}, how many would there be?
This would fit a different question about sharing {label:seraphina_total_pages.lowercase} into equal groups.

recap.headline: Why the reading log reached {quantity:combined_reading_pages}
causalChain: The storm added {quantity:afternoon_pages} to Seraphina's reading.

dataQuestion.prompt: What does {quantity:afternoon_pages} represent in Seraphina's reading model?
dataQuestion.correctFeedback: Right. {quantity:afternoon_pages} is the extra reading from the stormy afternoon.
dataQuestion.incorrectFeedback: That amount is the extra reading from the stormy afternoon: {quantity:afternoon_pages}.
